data.title: Severity-only review schema: remove scores and boolean from review output
data.description:
## Context
Code reviewer sub-agents, sprint orchestrators, and the commit workflow produce and consume a JSON review output (reviewer-findings.json) with three overlapping pass/fail signals: a scores object (5 numeric dimensions, each 1-5), a REVIEW_RESULT boolean line, and findings[].severity levels (critical/important/fragile/minor). When sub-agents write these values inconsistently — such as REVIEW_RESULT: passed alongside a critical finding, or score: 4 on a dimension with an important finding — engineers' commits are incorrectly blocked or silently passed. Today's validation tries to enforce consistency between these signals, but the root cause is structural redundancy: three ways to say the same thing invites contradictions.

## Success Criteria
- The reviewer-findings.json schema requires exactly two top-level keys: findings and summary. The scores object is removed from the schema, validation, and all reviewer agent definitions.
- The reviewer agent output contract no longer includes REVIEW_RESULT or MIN_SCORE lines. The remaining output lines are REVIEWER_HASH, FINDING_COUNT, and FILES.
- record-review.sh derives STATUS (passed/failed) exclusively from findings[].severity: any finding with severity critical, important, or fragile produces failed; only minor findings or no findings produce passed. No numeric score computation occurs.
- The review-status file no longer contains a score= line. The gate hook (pre-commit-review-gate.sh) continues to read line 1 (passed/failed) and diff_hash= unchanged.
- The gate never blocks based on text content of finding descriptions — only the structured severity field determines blocking behavior. A minor finding whose description contains the word "critical" or "important" does not trigger a block.
- All existing severity levels (critical, important, fragile, minor) retain their current blocking/non-blocking semantics: critical/important/fragile = blocking, minor = non-blocking.

## Dependencies
None

## Approach
Remove the scores object and REVIEW_RESULT/MIN_SCORE from the reviewer output schema entirely. Make findings[].severity the single source of truth for pass/fail determination. Update record-review.sh to derive STATUS purely from severity enumeration, and propagate the schema change through all 11 reviewer agent files (including 4 overlay agents), reviewer-base.md, code-review-dispatch.md, validate-review-output.sh, resolve-overlay-findings.sh, review-fix-dispatch.md, REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md, and sprint SKILL.md LLM-instruction references.

## Scenario Analysis
4 scenarios survived blue team filtering:
- resolve-overlay-findings.sh scores construction (high, runtime): script constructs scores from severity mappings; must be updated to omit scores.
- review-fix-dispatch.md references scores (medium, configuration): line 74 references scores object; must extract findings array only.
- Overlay sub-agents not updated (critical, configuration): security/performance/test-quality overlay agents emit REVIEW_RESULT and scores; must be included in migration scope.
- Plugin cache retains old schema (high, deployment): stale plugin cache may retain old agent definitions; include explicit cache resync step.

## Prompt Alignment Findings
- Dimensional coverage collapse (moderate-high risk): add mandatory pre-output checklist instruction requiring agents to confirm consideration of all 5 categories.
- Deep-tier specialist scope drift (high risk): update specialist delta files with explicit negative constraints; category field on findings provides scope attribution.
